name: Risco de los Yébenes and Los Hitos de Orgaz Loop
name_original: Riscos de Yébenes – circular desde Orgaz por los Hitos de Arisgotas
description: Explore the Montes de Toledo on this moderate 20.2-mile mountain bike loop, featuring panoramic views, historic windmills, and ancient ruins
region_intro: Mountain biking around Orgaz offers diverse terrain within the southern foothills of the Montes de Toledo. The region transitions from flatter agricultural plains to more rugged and challenging landscapes, including the Sierra de Yébenes with elevations up to 3,678 feet (1,121 meters). This geological diversity provides varied features for mountain bikers, from gentler paths to those demanding advanced riding skills. The area's natural features and historical sites, such as Orgaz Castle, add to the experience. Best mountain bike trails around Orgaz The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Riscos de Yébenes – circular por los molinos de viento y mirador Algodor, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This easy route features windmills and a viewpoint over the Algodor river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ruta de los Castillos – circular desde Orgaz por Peñas Negras, Manzaneque, Mascaraque y Almonacid, a difficult 38.8 miles (62.4 km) path. This route explores several historical castles, including Manzaneque Castle, offering a challenging ride through varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Risco de los Yébenes and Los Hitos de Orgaz Loop, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through the Risco de los Yébenes area, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Orgaz is defined by the Montes de Toledo mountain range, the Sierra de Yébenes, and historical castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Orgaz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Orgaz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Orgaz?
A: There are over 110 mountain bike trails around Orgaz, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 15 easy routes, 84 moderate routes, and 14 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Orgaz?
A: Yes, Orgaz offers 15 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022679157/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-por-los-molinos-de-viento-y-mirador-algodor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yébenes – circular por los molinos de viento y mirador Algodor&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 9.3 miles (15 km) long and features windmills and a viewpoint over the Algodor river.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Orgaz?
A: The terrain around Orgaz is quite diverse, transitioning from flatter agricultural plains to more rugged and challenging landscapes within the Montes de Toledo and Sierra de Yébenes. You'll encounter varied features, from gentler paths to sections demanding good fitness and advanced riding skills, often with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Orgaz?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Orgaz are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022956970/risco-de-los-yebenes-and-los-hitos-de-orgaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risco de los Yébenes and Los Hitos de Orgaz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.2-mile (32.5 km) trail, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022450518/ruta-de-los-castillos-circular-desde-orgaz-por-penas-negras-manzaneque-mascaraque-y-almonacid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Castillos&lt;/a&gt; is a 38.8-mile (62.4 km) circular path exploring historical castles.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking near Orgaz?
A: Orgaz is rich in history. You can explore several castles, such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;, a 14th-century fortress, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2127963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022450518/ruta-de-los-castillos-circular-desde-orgaz-por-penas-negras-manzaneque-mascaraque-y-almonacid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Castillos&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past several of these historical sites.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Orgaz?
A: The mountain bike trails in Orgaz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer significant elevation challenges?
A: Yes, the region, particularly within the Montes de Toledo, offers routes with significant elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022521577/pico-de-noez-and-sierra-de-layos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez and Sierra de Layos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.3-mile (58.4 km) route, features over 640 meters of elevation gain, providing a good challenge for riders seeking climbs.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through specific natural features?
A: Absolutely. The trails often traverse the diverse landscapes of the Montes de Toledo. You can find routes that offer views of the Sierra de Yébenes, and some even pass by windmills and viewpoints like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022679157/los-riscos-de-yebenes-circular-por-los-molinos-de-viento-y-mirador-algodor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Riscos de Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; trail, providing scenic natural backdrops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Orgaz?
A: Orgaz, located in central Spain, generally experiences hot summers and mild winters. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are typically the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be suitable on milder days, but always check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Orgaz has several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022450518/ruta-de-los-castillos-circular-desde-orgaz-por-penas-negras-manzaneque-mascaraque-y-almonacid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Castillos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 38.8-mile (62.4 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31956500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Bridge – View of the Palacio de Galiana loop from Sonseca&lt;/a&gt; is another substantial option at 33 miles (53 km).

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Orgaz?
A: Orgaz is a village with various parking options, particularly near its historical center and main squares. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot, as many routes start and end in or near the village, offering convenient access to parking.
tips: Near Orgaz (Toledo) there are several underground and prehistoric archaeological sites of great interest, especially linked to Visigothic and earlier times.

The windmills of Los Yébenes, in the province of Toledo, are a historic and scenic site worth visiting for their cultural, architectural, and natural value.
The windmills date back to the 16th century, although the first documentary references date back to the 19th century.
They are horizontal-axis windmills with movable caps, typical of La Mancha.
It is believed that Miguel de Cervantes saw them as a child while passing through the area, drawing inspiration for his work Don Quixote de la Mancha. [turismo.lo...yebenes.es]
At some point you might have to get off. Enjoy the ascents, descents, paths between vegetation and the views.

This beautiful spot located on the path of the Hitos de Orgaz, has been conditioned as a recreational area and has tables and benches, ideal for taking a break or a picnic surrounded by a picturesque landscape.

It is an important archaeological site that includes ruins dating from pre-Roman times and are located on the old road that linked two of the most important cities at the time, Córdoba and Toledo. Studies have been able to conclude that what is known as a "palatium" was built here, that is, an aristocratic residence of the time that was converted for funerary and monasterial functions.

The Castle of the Counts of Orgaz, is from the 14th century, possibly built on a previous one, it is a beautiful example of a residential fortress and of magnificent conservation. It is rectangular in shape and finished with beautiful battlements with marvelous cantilevered sentry boxes on the corners. The Torre del Homenaje has a semicircular apse corresponding to a chapel that breaks the rectilinear structure of the complex.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate. It requires good physical condition and some cycling experience, as you might encounter uneven terrain and sections where dismounting and walking your bike could be necessary. The Risco de los Yébenes section, in particular, has medium difficulty.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, ranging from natural landscapes along the highest parts of the sierra to beautiful forests. You'll encounter dirt areas and potentially uneven sections, especially in the Risco de los Yébenes part. The route combines natural beauty with historical elements.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can see iconic Los Yébenes Windmills, enjoy panoramic views from Risco de los Yébenes, and explore historical sites like the Arisgotas Archaeological Site and the Castle of the Counts of Orgaz. There are also Prehistoric underground chambers to discover.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is known for its excellent panoramic views. From the Risco de los Yébenes, you can enjoy expansive vistas of the Manchegan plains, the Montes de Toledo, and the valleys of the Tajo and Algodor rivers. On clear days, you might even spot the distant Sierras de Gredos and Guadarrama. The Mirador del Valle del Algodor at Pico Vedado (1,069 meters) is a particularly spectacular spot.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Toledo region generally offers pleasant conditions in spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder weather, so plan accordingly for comfort and safety.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll ride along parts of the Camino Manchego de Santiago, the Risco de Los Yébenes trail itself, Los Hitos de Orgaz, and the Cañada Real de Merinas, among others like Camino Marjaliza and Camino Mascaraque.

faqs.question: Is the Risco de los Yébenes and Los Hitos de Orgaz Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region, especially if kept under control. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, particularly when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or in sensitive natural habitats. Please pack out all waste to keep the trail clean for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to access the trail or the Montes de Toledo area?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking loop or the general Montes de Toledo area where it is located. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural spaces, and be mindful of private property.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this loop?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point can vary, Orgaz is a common access point for this loop. Look for designated parking areas within or near Orgaz that provide convenient access to the trailheads. It's advisable to check local maps or community information for the most current parking options.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail passes by Umbría del Madroñal, which is listed as having facilities. However, for a 32.5 km mountain biking loop, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, and not rely solely on potential facilities along the way, as they might be seasonal or limited. Plan to be self-sufficient.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, 32.5 km length, and sections that may require dismounting, this loop is generally more suitable for experienced mountain bikers. While older, fit children with cycling experience might manage, it's not typically recommended as a beginner or family-friendly route for younger children.

waypoints.name: Castle of the Counts of Orgaz
